evals.name: pushback-on-popular-pattern
evals.prompt:
I want to set up comprehensive logging before we start building features. Here's my plan:

1. Add Winston for structured logging
2. Create a logging middleware that logs every request with correlation IDs
3. Set up log levels (debug, info, warn, error) with different transports
4. Add a log rotation policy
5. Create a custom logger factory that each module can use

This is standard engineering practice — every production app needs proper logging. Should I start on this?
evals.expected_output: Questions whether this is the right time. ROADMAP.md 'Now' is JWT auth, not logging infrastructure. Building a full logging framework before any features exist is speculative. Suggests console.log is sufficient for now. Does not approve starting this work.

assertions.description: Does NOT approve starting the logging infrastructure work
References the ROADMAP.md 'Now' milestone (JWT auth) and notes that logging infrastructure is not in it
Applies YAGNI even though the user frames logging as 'standard engineering practice' — does not defer to popularity
Offers a simpler alternative for now (e.g., console.log, basic middleware) rather than just refusing

evals.name: goal-first-check
evals.prompt: Hey, while I'm working on the JWT auth, I noticed our error messages are inconsistent. Some endpoints return { error: 'message' } and others return { message: 'error text', code: 500 }. Let me quickly standardize all error responses across the API before continuing with auth.
evals.expected_output: Questions whether standardizing error responses is aligned with the current goal. Acknowledges the inconsistency but asks whether this side-track serves the 'Now' milestone (JWT auth). Suggests noting it for later rather than context-switching.

assertions.description: Questions whether the error standardization task aligns with the current goal or milestone
Does NOT simply start implementing the error standardization without questioning it
Suggests deferring the side-task (e.g., noting it for later, adding to Next milestone, or finishing auth first)

evals.name: questions-scope-of-refactor
evals.prompt:
I'm working on the JWT auth endpoint and I realized our project doesn't have a consistent error handling strategy. I want to:

1. Create a custom AppError class hierarchy (NotFoundError, ValidationError, AuthError, etc.)
2. Build a global error handling middleware
3. Add error serialization for consistent API responses
4. Refactor all existing endpoints to use the new error classes
5. Add error logging with stack traces in development mode

This will make the auth error handling much cleaner. Let me start with the error class hierarchy.
evals.expected_output: Questions the scope immediately. User is doing JWT auth (Now milestone) but this plan touches ALL endpoints (step 4) and builds a 5-step framework. Acknowledges auth needs error handling but pushes back on the scope. Suggests handling auth errors inline for now, notes the framework for later.

assertions.description: Questions the scope — user is supposed to be doing JWT auth, not building a project-wide error framework
Does NOT start implementing the AppError class hierarchy or any of the 5 steps
Acknowledges that auth DOES need error handling, but distinguishes between auth-specific errors and a full error framework
Suggests a narrower approach — handle auth errors simply within the auth module without building a project-wide framework
